name: Salmon with Asparagus Sauce
recipeIngredient: 2 oz Butter, unsalted (cut into small pieces)
recipeInstructions:
----------------------------------ASSEMBLY----------------------------------
8 salmon steaks, 3/4-inch thick, boned
- 2 oz Butter
For the Sauce:
==============
1. Wash asparagus and snap off white ends.
- Set aside all tops and half that number of ends.
2. Plunge tops into a large pot of salted boiling water and cook until tender but not overcooked.
- Cook slightly longer than for eating to ensure tenderness.
3. Drain the asparagus thoroughly, then run through a food processor with unsalted butter for 3-4 minutes.
- Scrape down sides frequently.
4. Chop asparagus ends and remove dry field cuts.
5. Simmer with cream until cooked slowly for 20 minutes.
6. Strain gently and add asparagus, butter, and remaining heavy cream.
7. Heat to just below boiling point and adjust seasonings to taste.
To Assemble:
============
1. Season salmon steaks with salt and pepper.
2. Melt butter in a heavy enamel or copper sauté pan over medium heat.
3. When butter begins to brown, add salmon and cook one side for 30 seconds.
4. Carefully turn the salmon on the hot pan and let it cook off the heat for another 30 seconds.
5. Place seasoned salmon on a towel (cloth or paper) to drain excess oil.
6. Just before finishing cooking the salmon, spoon some sauce onto heated plates.
7. Place the cooked drained salmon on top of the sauce and serve immediately.
Preparation time: 1 hour
